frameworks.name: divisive normalization
frameworks.one_line: Neuron's activity is scaled by activity of a larger neuronal pool.

frameworks.name: canonical computation
frameworks.one_line: Shared computational motifs across circuits, regions, and species.

core_question: Does divisive normalization serve as a canonical computation for neural information coding across brain regions and modalities?
key_assumptions: Common computational motifs recur across neural circuits
Neural coding can be characterized by input-output transformations
Contextual modulation arises from pooled neuronal activity
key_findings: Divisive normalization documented across brain regions, hierarchical levels, and sensory modalities
Normalization extends beyond sensory processing to attention, multisensory integration, and decision making
interpretations: Divisive normalization qualifies as a canonical computation introducing intrinsic contextual modulation into coding
